name: Slow Cooked Turkey Breast with Mushroom
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup sherry or dry white wine
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse the turkey breast and drain well. Pat dry with a paper towel.
2. Place the turkey, skin side up, in the slow cooker.
3. Brush the turkey with butter and sprinkle with parsley, tarragon, and paprika.
4. Add wine, salt, and white pepper to the slow cooker.
5. Cover and cook on high setting for 1 hour.
6. Reduce heat to low and continue cooking for 6 to 7 hours until the internal temperature of the breast reaches 170°F.
7. Slice and serve with mushroom sauce.
MUSHROOM SAUCE
1. Strain the drippings from the slow cooker into a large liquid measure.
2. Remove any remaining fat.
3. Add mushrooms to the drippings and enough water or sherry wine to make 1 - 3/4 cupfuls.
4. Dissolve cornstarch in water or wine and stir into the drippings.
5. Pour into a saucepan and add tarragon, salt, and white pepper to taste.
6. Cook over medium to low heat, stirring constantly, until thickened and clear.
